lines: * Tests the explain command with the maxTimeMS option.
assert.neq(null, standalone, "mongod was unable to start up");
-// Set fail point to make sure operations with "maxTimeMS" set will time out.
+// The shell explain helpers hoist a nested maxTimeMS up to the top level, so they would not exercise
+// the path where maxTimeMS lives inside the explained command. Issue explain via raw db.runCommand
+// to verify the server honors maxTimeMS in both cases across a range of explained commands.
+    {mapReduce: collName, map: mapFn, reduce: reduceFn, out: destCollName},
+// With the fail point on, any operation that has a deadline set times out immediately.
+            // A maxTimeMS nested inside the explained command times out.
+            // A top-level maxTimeMS on the explain command times out.
+// With no fail point and no maxTimeMS, or a nested maxTimeMS that is not exceeded, explain succeeds.
-    // Expect explain to time out if "maxTimeMS" is set on the aggregate command.
-    // Expect explain to time out if "maxTimeMS" is set on the count command.
-    // Expect explain to time out if "maxTimeMS" is set on the distinct command.
-    // Expect explain to time out if "maxTimeMS" is set on the find command.
-    // Expect explain to time out if "maxTimeMS" is set on the findAndModify command.
-    // Expect explain to time out if "maxTimeMS" is set on the mapReduce command.
+// When both are provided, the smaller of the two maxTimeMS values should be honored. Block the explain long enough that only the
+// smaller deadline is exceeded: the larger value on its own would not time out, so a timeout shows
+// the smaller value was the one applied.
+        // The top-level value is the smaller one.
+        // The nested value is the smaller one.
+ * Verifies that mongos honors a maxTimeMS nested inside an explained command issued via raw
+ * db.runCommand, matching the behavior of a top-level maxTimeMS.
+describe("explain maxTimeMS via raw db.runCommand on mongos", function () {
+    it("times out for both top-level and nested maxTimeMS", () => {
+        // Force any operation that has a deadline set on the mongos to time out immediately.
+    it("succeeds when a nested maxTimeMS is not exceeded", () => {
+            // Fold a maxTimeMS nested inside the explained command into this command's own
+            // maxTimeMS so the standard deadline machinery enforces it like a top-level maxTimeMS.
+    // Neither value imposes a positive limit, so both are 0 ("no limit") or unset. An explicit 0
+    // must be preserved (unlike an unset value) so it bypasses defaultMaxTimeMS. Keep the explain
+    // value when it is set, otherwise fall back to the nested value (an explicit 0, or unset).
BSONObj makeExplainedObjForMongos(const BSONObj& outerObj, const BSONObj& innerObj) {
BSONObj makeExplainedObjForMongos(const BSONObj& outerObj, const BSONObj& innerObj);
+ * Resolves the effective maxTimeMS for an explain command from the explain command's own maxTimeMS
+ * and a maxTimeMS nested inside the explained command. When both are positive the smaller (more
+ * restrictive) value wins. A value of 0 means "no limit" and never overrides a positive value; an
+ * explicit 0 in either placement is preserved (rather than collapsing to "unset") so it bypasses
+ * defaultMaxTimeMS. The result is unset only when both placements are unset. Folding the result
+ * back into the explain command's maxTimeMS lets the standard deadline machinery enforce a nested
+ * maxTimeMS exactly like a top-level one.

+    // An explicit 0 ("no limit") must survive even when the other placement is unset, so the
+    // explain command keeps an explicit maxTimeMS and does not inherit defaultMaxTimeMS.
+            // Fold a maxTimeMS nested inside the explained command into this command's own
+            // maxTimeMS so the standard deadline machinery enforces it like a top-level maxTimeMS.
